text: Whatever you do, work at it with all your heart, as working for the Lord.
I can do all this through him who gives me strength.
She sets about her work vigorously; her arms are strong for her tasks.
Do you not know that your bodies are temples of the Holy Spirit?
The Lord is my strength and my shield; my heart trusts in him, and he helps me.
Give thanks in all circumstances; for this is God's will for you in Christ Jesus.
Let us not become weary in doing good, for at the proper time we will reap a harvest.
Taste and see that the Lord is good; blessed is the one who takes refuge in him.
For physical training is of some value, but godliness has value for all things.
The joy of the Lord is your strength.
Commit to the Lord whatever you do, and he will establish your plans.
Be strong and courageous. Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged.
Enter his gates with thanksgiving and his courts with praise.
A cheerful heart is good medicine, but a crushed spirit dries up the bones.
He gives strength to the weary and increases the power of the weak.
No discipline seems pleasant at the time, but painful. Later it produces peace.
I praise you because I am fearfully and wonderfully made.
Run in such a way as to get the prize.
The Lord is my strength and my defense; he has become my salvation.
Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good; his love endures forever.
Watch and pray so that you will not fall into temptation.
Those who hope in the Lord will renew their strength. They will soar on wings like eagles.
And whatever you do, whether in word or deed, do it all in the name of the Lord Jesus.
Let everything that has breath praise the Lord.
Bless the Lord, O my soul, and forget not all his benefits.
The name of the Lord is a fortified tower; the righteous run to it and are safe.
Consider it pure joy when you face trials, because the testing of your faith produces perseverance.
He has shown you what is good: to act justly, to love mercy, and to walk humbly with your God.
Shout for joy to the Lord, all the earth.
Be on your guard; stand firm in the faith; be courageous; be strong.
Teach us to number our days, that we may gain a heart of wisdom.
The Lord is my shepherd, I lack nothing.
But those who hope in the Lord will renew their strength.
In peace I will lie down and sleep, for you alone, Lord, make me dwell in safety.
Therefore, I urge you, brothers and sisters, in view of God's mercy, to offer your bodies as a living sacrifice.
Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind.
I will sing the Lord's praise, for he has been good to me.
The Lord is my light and my salvation—whom shall I fear?
Let us run with perseverance the race marked out for us.
Every good and perfect gift is from above.
